name: ExtensionMolecularSequence_Focus
title: R5: What the molecular sequence is about, when it is not about the subject of record (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MolecularSequence.focus` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MolecularSequence.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MolecularSequence.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MolecularSequence.focus` has a context of MolecularSequence based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MolecularSequence`.
Element `MolecularSequence.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: What the molecular sequence is about, when it is not about the subject of record (new)
element.comment:
Element `MolecularSequence.focus` has a context of MolecularSequence based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MolecularSequence`.
Element `MolecularSequence.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: What the molecular sequence is about, when it is not about the subject of record
element.definition: The actual focus of a molecular sequence when it is not the patient of record representing something or someone associated with the patient such as a spouse, parent, child, or sibling. For example, in trio testing, the subject would be the child (proband) and the focus would be the parent.
